title: Consumer Price Index, Northeast region — March 2026
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The Northeast region's Consumer Price Index for March 2026 was 344.142, up 3.6 percent from March 2025. Energy prices surged 10.1 percent over the past year, wh
excerpt: The Northeast region's Consumer Price Index for March 2026 was 344.142, up 3.6 percent from March 2025. Energy prices surged 10.1 percent over the past year, while food prices rose 3.3 percent. The overall index increased 0.9 percent in March 2026.

content.introduction: The Northeast region's Consumer Price Index for March 2026 was 344.142, up 3.6 percent from March 2025. Energy prices surged 10.1 percent over the past year, while food prices rose 3.3 percent. The overall index increased 0.9 percent in March 2026.
body: The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) for Boston-Cambridge-Newton was up 0.7 percent from January to March, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported today. Phinney noted that the indexes for all items less food and energy (+0.5 percent), food (+1.4 percent), and energy (+1.6 percent) advanced over the bi-monthly period. The Boston area all items CPI-U advanced 2.0 percent for the 12 months ending in March. The index for all items less food and energy rose 1.7 percent. Over the same period, the indexes for food and energy increased 2.5 percent and 4.9 percent, respectively. Line chart of 12-month percent changes in the Consumer Price Index CPI-U for all items and all items less food and energy for Boston-Cambridge-Newton, MA-NH, March 2023 to March 2026, not seasonally adjusted. The index for all items less food and energy rose 1.7 percent over the year. The index for all items less food and energy rose 0.5 percent from January to March.

The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in the New York-Newark-Jersey City area rose 0.8 percent in March, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported today. Area prices up 0.8 percent over the month, 4.0 percent over the year. The New York area all items CPI-U advanced 4.0 percent for the 12 months ending in March. The index for all items less food and energy rose 3.3 percent. Over the same period, the energy index rose 17.1 percent, and the food index increased 3.3 percent. Line chart of 12-month percent changes in the Consumer Price Index CPI-U for all items and all items less food and energy for New York-Newark-Jersey City, NY-NJ-PA, March 2023 to March 2026, not seasonally adjusted. Food prices rose 0.2 percent for the month of March. The index for all items less food and energy increased 0.3 percent in March. The index for all items less food and energy advanced 3.3 percent over the year.

The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in the Northeast Region advanced 0.9 percent in March, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported today. Area prices were up 0.9 percent over the past month, up 3.6 percent from a year ago. The energy index increased 10.1 percent. The Northeast Region all items CPI-U rose 3.6 percent for the 12 months ending in March. The index for all items less food and energy advanced 2.9 percent over the year. Food prices advanced 3.3 percent. Line chart of 12-month percent changes in the CPI-U in the Northeast region from March 2023 to March 2026. Over-the-year percent change in CPI-U, Northeast region, March 2023–March 2026. Food prices advanced 0.1 percent for the month of March. Food prices advanced 3.3 percent over the year. The index for all items less food and energy advanced 0.4 percent in March. The index for all items less food and energy rose 2.9 percent over the year.
